institutions.name: TBRC Business Research Private Limited (trading as 'The Business Research Company', publisher of record on researchandmarkets.com)

entries.useful_lead: Global downhole tools market claimed at $5.79B (2025) rising to $6.07B (2026) and $7.33B by 2030 (4.7-4.8% CAGR); independently confirm via a non-Indian-origin market-research firm, oilfield-services trade association, or public-company investor materials before citing any downhole-tools market-size figure.
entries.notes: The distribution marketplace (researchandmarkets.com) is Dublin, Ireland-based, but the report's actual producing/authoring firm attributed on the listing page is 'The Business Research Company.' That brand's UK (Canary Wharf) and US (Media, PA) addresses are marketing offices; the controlling legal entity per the site's own copyright notice and CPHI/company-registry records is TBRC Business Research Private Limited, CIN U93000TG2014PTC093061, incorporated 19-Feb-2014, registered office Jubilee Hills, Hyderabad, Telangana, India (ROC Hyderabad). No matching 'The Business Research Company Ltd' entity was found in UK Companies House. This matches the audit directive's flagged pattern of India-registered consultancies using US/UK virtual-office branding; the source is downgraded to discovery_only regardless of the marketplace's own Irish domicile.
All 3 authors (O'Connor, Talmor, Yagoobi) resolved to Worcester Polytechnic Institute, US. No IN affiliation.

University of Chinese Academy of Sciences

entries.notes: All 6 authors resolved to Chinese institutions. No IN affiliation.

DATA-QUALITY FLAG (not an origin issue): both OpenAlex and Crossref register this DOI's title as 'A high-performance solid-state electrocaloric cooling system' with all 8 authors at PARC/Xerox (Palo Alto, US) and Murata Manufacturing (Kyoto, Japan) -- this does not match the ledger's listed authorship 'Qian, Suxin et al.; UCLA-affiliated.' Flagging for the orchestrator to reconcile the authorship field separately; the origin verdict is unaffected either way, since neither the Crossref-registered authors (US/Japan) nor a UCLA-based author set would be Indian.
